title: Pre-install PyYAML to prevent torch-npu transitive dependency failure
symptom: ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'yaml' — triggers immediately on `import torch` or `import torch_npu`. The full traceback points to torch_npu/npu/_memory_viz.py line 11 (`import yaml`), cascading through torch's _import_device_backends into a complete torch import failure.
root_cause: torch-npu 2.5.1's memory visualization submodule (torch_npu/npu/_memory_viz.py) imports yaml unconditionally at module load time. However, PyYAML is not declared in torch-npu's pip metadata (requires field), so a fresh virtual environment with only torch-npu installed will lack it. The error is misleading because the traceback originates from `import torch` (not `import torch_npu`), making the actual missing dependency hard to diagnose.
fix_steps: 1. Before or alongside installing torch-npu, explicitly install PyYAML: `pip install PyYAML` (use domestic mirror if needed: `pip install PyYAML -i https://mirrors.aliyun.com/pypi/simple/`).
2. Alternatively, add PyYAML to the project's requirements.txt alongside torch, torch-npu, and torchvision.
3. After installing torch-npu, run a pre-flight validation: `python -c 'import torch_npu; import torch_npu.npu'` — if this exits cleanly without ModuleNotFoundError, the dependency is resolved.
4. If the error still occurs, verify the yaml module is accessible: `python -c 'import yaml; print(yaml.__version__)'`.
affected_patterns: import torch  # fails with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'yaml' when torch-npu is installed but PyYAML is not
import torch_npu  # fails at torch_npu/npu/_memory_viz.py line 11
antipatterns: Do NOT attempt to patch torch_npu internal files (_memory_viz.py) — the file is overwritten on package upgrade.
Do NOT confuse this error with a torch installation issue — the traceback starting at 'import torch' is a red herring; the actual failure is in torch_npu backend loading.
Do NOT use CPU fallback as a workaround — this is a hard dependency failure, not a performance issue.

meta.refiner_reasoning: This is a definitive, easily-reproducible issue on any fresh venv with torch-npu 2.5.1. The misleading traceback (originating from 'import torch' rather than 'import torch_npu') makes it costly to debug without prior knowledge. Pre-installing PyYAML completely prevents the issue. Verified on CANN 8.1.RC1, driver 25.0.rc1.1, Python 3.10.17.

problem_description: torch-npu 2.5.1's memory visualization submodule (_memory_viz.py) imports yaml unconditionally, but PyYAML is not declared in the package's pip metadata. When creating a fresh venv, torch import fails with ModuleNotFoundError: No module named 'yaml' during torch_npu backend loading, cascading into complete torch import failure.
rough_fix_approach: Pre-install PyYAML in the venv before using torch-npu: pip install PyYAML -i https://mirrors.aliyun.com/pypi/simple/. Alternatively, pin requirements.txt to include PyYAML alongside torch and torch-npu.
